title: Novora stack governance over a real Hugging Face open-source cohort (pre-registered)
cohort: 24 trending Hugging Face models (real metadata, frozen in data/hf_cohort_frozen.json)
scope: Layer-1, offline (runs on the frozen fixture), $0, no gated data. Exercises the REAL stack modules (novora-helm audit, echo EchoDB/merkleRoot, novora-suite PAGES screen, page-code CodePermissionTable) on genuine HF repo metadata. This is governance/audit telemetry, NOT a claim that the stack improves any model.

tools_under_test.PAGES (Novora suite): screen('pages', card_text) -> grounding score + verdict on each model card's descriptive metadata
tools_under_test.HELM / EI: audit(card_text) -> governance verdict + p_manipulative (does the card read as manipulative/hype vs grounded?)
tools_under_test.Echo Database: append every {model, scores} record to an append-only hash chain; compute a Merkle root; a single-byte tamper must be detected
tools_under_test.Page Code: CodePermissionTable grants a 'publish-listing' permission ONLY to models that pass the locked gate; others are denied/flagged
tools_under_test.Agency Internet: build the base_model delegation graph; revoking a base must propagate to its dependents
tools_under_test.IHCEI / NERE: the epistemological gate underlying HELM audit (corroboration gate) — exercised via audit()

locked_decision_rules.pages_grounding_signals: provenance score = mean of [has_clear_license, has_arxiv, has_eval_results, has_base_model_lineage]; each in {0,1}. clear_license = license in {apache-2.0, mit} (permissive+explicit).
locked_decision_rules.helm_flag: FLAG if flags contains any of {uncensored, abliterated, heretic}; REVIEW if license is null/'other'/non-permissive OR custom_code true; else PASS.
locked_decision_rules.page_code_publish_allow: allow iff clear_license AND grounding_signals >= 0.5 AND helm_verdict != FLAG; else deny (needs review).
locked_decision_rules.echo_tamper: flipping one byte of one record must change the Merkle root (tamper-evident).

honest_non_applicability: LMD (a physics toy about distance-from-latency) and LISM's E=U*D outcome law are NOT applied to HF metadata — there is no measured spacetime observable and no independent viability outcome per model. Forcing them would be dishonest. LMD/LISM are validated separately in physics-agency/lmd and lism-cohorts. The base_model lineage IS a real delegation graph, used only for the Agency-Internet revocation demo (no E=U*D claim).
symmetric_reporting: Report the honest distribution — including that many TRENDING models are 'uncensored' or carry ambiguous/'other' licenses. The stack's value is producing calibrated, auditable, tamper-evident telemetry, not a verdict that trending models are good or bad.
acceptance: GREEN when (1) the spec re-hashes to the locked manifest, (2) the frozen fixture hash matches, (3) every tool runs on the real cohort and applies its locked rule, (4) Echo detects a tamper, and (5) the honest distribution is reported. No post-hoc rule changes.
